exploration_notes: The root cause is in numpy/_core/_methods.py lines 159-160: the _var() function uses issubclass(arr.dtype.type, (nt.integer, nt.bool)) to decide whether to upcast integers to float64. Pandas nullable integer types (Int64Dtype) do not inherit from numpy.integer, so this check fails silently — variance is computed without float upcasting, causing truncation/overflow. The same pattern appears in _mean() (lines 126-127) and in the computation path selection (line 189). Contributing factors include p
